message:
test(fuzz): add derive_session_keys binding-properties target (resolves k56mo)

Adds fuzz_derive_session_keys covering fcp_protocol::derive_session_keys
(session.rs:858) — the handshake KDF that ties matching session keys
to the negotiated transcript. Existing fuzz_session_transcript only
calls the function and discards results.

Five metamorphic relations asserted:

  1. Determinism — same inputs yield identical SessionKeys.
  2. Direction-key inequality — k_mac_i2r ≠ k_mac_r2i ≠ k_ctx.
     The HKDF expansion produces 96 bytes split 32/32/32; identical
     slices at distinct offsets imply either HKDF collision
     (cryptographically infeasible) or slice-range overlap (real
     regression). The direction-blind variant collapses the
     reflection-attack defense session_metamorphic MR-MAC-4 catches
     from the verify side.
  3. Hello-nonce binding — bit-flipping hello_nonce changes the
     derived keys.
  4. Ack-nonce binding — same property for ack_nonce.
  5. Initiator↔responder swap — swapping the two node IDs MUST
     change the keys. The HKDF info string encodes them in order,
     so a direction-symmetric KDF would let peer A reuse peer B's
     key derivation (impersonation surface).

Bails on low-order DH outcomes (covered by fuzz_x25519_dh / hlnm4)
since that branch is not what this target probes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -90,6 +90,13 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_derive_session_keys"
+path = "fuzz_targets/derive_session_keys.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_fcps_frame"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fcps_frame.rs"
